channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: One clinic tracks the heavy toll Trump's immigration crackdown takes on mental health
item.description: Zocalo Health, a clinic in Los Angeles, screens all its patients for depression, anxiety and suicidal thoughts. It documented a marked increase in those conditions since ICE enforcement actions began.
item.content:encoded:
Zocalo Health, a clinic in Los Angeles, screens all its patients for depression, anxiety and suicidal thoughts. It documented a marked increase in those conditions since ICE enforcement actions began.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee

item.title: The foreign fighters who helped topple Assad — and why China worries about them
item.description: Thousands of Uyghurs became key fighters against Syria's Assad regime. For the first time, they agreed to be interviewed. NPR spent weeks with some of them to understand why they fled China for Syria.
item.content:encoded:
Thousands of Uyghurs became key fighters against Syria's Assad regime. For the first time, they agreed to be interviewed. NPR spent weeks with some of them to understand why they fled China for Syria.

(Image credit: Emily Feng)
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: How a CEO and Trump donor is weaponizing tariffs against his rivals
item.description: Cambria CEO Marty Davis has successfully asked the U.S. government to put tariffs on quartz. His business competitors are crying foul.
item.content:encoded:
Cambria CEO Marty Davis has successfully asked the U.S. government to put tariffs on quartz. His business competitors are crying foul.

(Image credit: Richard Tsong-Taatarii/Star Tribune via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan

item.title: New burn bans and Trump's battle with immigration and DEI are impacting forest fires
item.description: Firefighters say setting fires on purpose is one of the best  ways to protect against massive wildfires later. But the Trump administration is banning or stalling preventative burning across the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
Firefighters say setting fires on purpose is one of the best  ways to protect against massive wildfires later. But the Trump administration is banning or stalling preventative burning across the U.S.

(Image credit: Chiara Eisner)
item.dc:creator: Chiara Eisner

item.title: New York Magazine reviewing work of writer facing plagiarism allegations
item.description: The high-profile writer, who has written a forthcoming book about New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani, is being investigated by the magazine after being called out for copying the work of other writers.
item.content:encoded:
The high-profile writer, who has written a forthcoming book about New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani, is being investigated by the magazine after being called out for copying the work of other writers.

(Image credit: YouTube)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Louisiana senator who voted to convict Trump loses Republican primary
item.description: Sen. Bill Cassidy is one of few remaining Republican senators who voted to convict President Trump after the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol. Trump endorsed his opponent, Rep. Julia Letlow.
item.content:encoded:
Sen. Bill Cassidy is one of few remaining Republican senators who voted to convict President Trump after the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol. Trump endorsed his opponent, Rep. Julia Letlow.

(Image credit: Annie Flanagan for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: Napoleon Solo holds off Iron Honor to win the Preakness
item.description: It's the colt's first victory of the year. Kentucky Derby winner Golden Tempo's exit from the race left the field of 14 horses wide open to contest the middle jewel of the Triple Crown.
item.content:encoded:
It's the colt's first victory of the year. Kentucky Derby winner Golden Tempo's exit from the race left the field of 14 horses wide open to contest the middle jewel of the Triple Crown.

(Image credit: Nick Wass)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Bulgarian banger 'Bangaranga' bags country its 1st Eurovision win
item.description: Bulgaria was not among the favorites to win. But performer Dara's catchy, banging anthem bested 24 other nations at the 70th edition of the international singing extravaganza.
item.content:encoded:
Bulgaria was not among the favorites to win. But performer Dara's catchy, banging anthem bested 24 other nations at the 70th edition of the international singing extravaganza.

(Image credit: Helmut Fohringer)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: World Health Organization declares Ebola outbreak in Congo a global health emergency
item.description: Health officials are working to contain a new Ebola outbreak in the DRC of a virus strain with no known vaccine.
item.content:encoded:
Health officials are working to contain a new Ebola outbreak in the DRC of a virus strain with no known vaccine.

(Image credit: Constant Same Bagalwa)
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin

item.title: North America poised for light display of aurora borealis
item.description: The splashy nighttime phenomenon will be best observed on Saturday and Sunday nights
item.content:encoded:
The splashy nighttime phenomenon will be best observed on Saturday and Sunday nights

(Image credit: Charlie Riedel)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise
